title: caltha palustris var. scaposa
intro: Caltha palustris var. scaposa, also known as marsh marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of marsh marigold include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of marsh marigold may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
